steps.title: Identify the clause structure risk
steps.content: The sentence contains a main independent clause (“ancient Egyptians slept on hard, elevated headrests”), followed by a comma and the noun phrase “their design.” The blank could be filled with either a form that creates a second independent clause or a form that creates a participial phrase.

steps.title: Apply the comma splice rule
steps.content:
”Prioritizing” creates a participial phrase elaborating on the design without creating a second independent clause.

Using any finite verb (prioritized, was prioritizing, had prioritized) creates an independent clause after the comma, causing a comma splice.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
